chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمَحْصُورِ وَ الْمَصْدُودِ وَ مَا عَلَيْهِمَا مِنَ الْكَفَّارَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 101 – The confined one and the prevented one, what is the expiation upon the both of them

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/334)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/334)
text: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) حِينَ صُدَّ بِالْحُدَيْبِيَةِ قَصَّرَ وَ أَحَلَّ وَ نَحَرَ ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ مِنْهَا وَ لَمْ يَجِبْ عَلَيْهِ الْحَلْقُ حَتَّى يَقْضِيَ النُّسُكَ فَأَمَّا الْمَحْصُورُ فَإِنَّمَا يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ التَّقْصِيرُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Abu Nasr, from Dawood Bin Sirham, from Abdullah Bin Farqad, from Humran,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said that Rasool-Allahsaww, when hesaww was prevented at Al-Hudaybiyya, shortened (hissaww nails, hair etc.), and freed from wearing Ihraam, and offered a sacrifice. Then hesaww left from it. And, the shaving (of the head) is not Obligated until the rituals are fulfilled. So, as for the confined one, rather the shortening (of hair, nails, etc.) happens to be upon him’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn abu Nasr from Dawud ibn Sarhan from ‘Abd Allah ibn Farqad from Humran who has said the following: “When the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause was prevented at al-Hudaybiyah from performing al-Hajj, he (the Messenger of Allah) did Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on). He (the Messenger of Allah) then came out of the state of Ihram and offered sacrifice, then left that place. Shaving was not obligatory for him until all acts of al-Hajj are performed. One who is Mahsur (confined) must carry out Taqsir (cutting some hair or finger nails and so on).’”
